what_it_does: This file is a ledger-style metadata record that registers an image attachment in the design-assets channel, pointing to a specific icon asset by a URI-like target and recording when it was added.
observations: YAML frontmatter only, no body content
Attachment id att-60033b52 with type images
standouts: The target uses a custom image:// URI scheme to reference an icon asset named Icon_ConceptV_V
It records an attachment event with precise timestamp and typed classification
conventions: Uses YAML frontmatter for structured metadata
Id prefix att- follows an attachment naming convention
concerns: The file contains only metadata and gives no description of the image content or purpose
The image:// target cannot be resolved or validated from within this file alone
No author, source, or versioning information is recorded
notes: A bare attachment ledger entry that links the design-assets channel to an icon asset called Icon_ConceptV_V.
questions: What does Icon_ConceptV_V represent visually or conceptually?
What image format, resolution, or size does the target refer to?
Who added this attachment and why?
purpose_vs_actual: The file appears to do exactly what an attachment ledger entry should do: record id, channel, type, target, and timestamp.
apparent_themes: Lightweight metadata pointers to external assets
intention_signals: It seems intended to create a durable, queryable record that an image asset belongs to the design-assets channel.
novelty: The use of a custom image:// URI scheme for the target is slightly distinct from a plain file path.
contribution: It contributes a typed, timestamped pointer that associates a named icon asset with the design-assets channel, which a fused system could use to locate or list channel attachments.
summary_for_embedding: A YAML-frontmatter attachment ledger entry for the design-assets channel that registers an images-type attachment pointing to image://design-assets/icons/Icon_ConceptV_V with an ISO 8601 timestamp.
intention_for_embedding: To record that an icon asset named Icon_ConceptV_V was attached to the design-assets channel at a specific time.
